title: Ctenitis sacholepis
intro: Ctenitis sachalepis is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help to alleviate conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Ctenitis sachalepis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are employed to support wound healing, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
taxonomy: Ctenitis sacholepis belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae, commonly known as the wood fern family. This species is a type of fern, specifically a member of the genus Ctenitis, which is native to tropical regions. Ctenitis sacholepis is a vascular plant, characterized by its fern-like leaves and reproductive structures. The classification of Ctenitis sacholepis is further supported by its morphological features, such as its leaf shape and venation pattern. This species is often found growing in shaded, humid areas, where it can thrive in a variety of environments.
